name: Rebordelo - Sanctuary of Our Lady of Health Circular
name_original: Carregue para o Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Saúde — circular de Rebordelo
description: Cycle the moderate 18.7-mile (30.0 km) Load for the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Saúde loop, gaining 2270 feet (692 metres) near Rebordelo.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Rebordelo traverse a diverse topography, featuring valleys, rolling countryside, and more mountainous terrain. The region, situated in Portugal's Bragança district, offers a serene environment with low traffic on rural roads. Cyclists can explore areas near the Ferreira River and the Montesinho Natural Park, which is known for its protected landscapes. This varied terrain provides options for both challenging climbs and tranquil rides. Best road cycling routes around Rebordelo The most popular road cycling route is From Valpaços to Mirandela — circular along the Rabaçal river, a 48.7 miles (78.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 49 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers substantial elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Rebordelo - Sanctuary of Our Lady of Health Circular, a moderate 18.7 miles (30.0 km) path. This route provides a scenic ride with notable elevation changes. Local road cyclists also love the Vale de Fontes and Agrochão Loop, a 22.0 miles (35.4 km) trail leading through rural countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Road cycling around Rebordelo is defined by diverse topography, rural tranquility, and riverine scenery, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Rebordelo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 30 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Rebordelo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Rebordelo?
A: There are 11 dedicated road cycling routes around Rebordelo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These include 6 moderate routes and 5 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Rebordelo?
A: The Rebordelo region features a diverse topography, ranging from valleys and rolling countryside to more mountainous terrain. You can expect a mix of challenging climbs and tranquil rides through serene rural landscapes, often with low traffic on the roads.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners or families?
A: While Rebordelo offers many moderate and difficult routes, there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the immediate area. However, the region's rural tranquility and low traffic can make some moderate routes more accessible for those looking for a less intense experience, especially through the rolling countryside.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401615313/from-valpacos-to-mirandela-circular-along-the-rabacal-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Valpaços to Mirandela — circular along the Rabaçal river&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial elevation gain over 78.4 km. The Montesinho Natural Park, nearby, is also known for its hard road rides with significant climbs.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the Rebordelo area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1399135195/rebordelo-sanctuary-of-our-lady-of-health-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rebordelo - Sanctuary of Our Lady of Health Circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401627699/vale-de-fontes-and-agrochao-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale de Fontes and Agrochão Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular choices among local cyclists.

Q: What natural features or scenic views can I expect to see while cycling?
A: You'll be immersed in nature, with routes often following the scenic Ferreira River through valleys and rural landscapes. The nearby Montesinho Natural Park offers breathtaking landscapes and vast oak forests. Further afield, the Tuela and Baceiro rivers provide engaging scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Rebordelo?
A: The region generally offers good conditions for cycling. While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, the tranquil rural environment suggests that spring and autumn would likely be ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters might be cooler, especially in higher elevations.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Rebordelo?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, the rural nature of Rebordelo and its surrounding areas often means that parking is available in or near villages, or at designated starting points for popular routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there options for food and drink near the cycling routes?
A: Rebordelo and nearby towns like Vinhais offer local cafes and pubs where you can refuel. Given the rural tranquility of many routes, it's a good idea to plan your stops in advance or carry sufficient provisions, especially on longer rides.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Rebordelo?
A: The road cycling routes in Rebordelo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse topography, rural tranquility, and riverine scenery that define the area's rides.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites I can visit while cycling?
A: Yes, nearby towns like Vinhais, situated on the side of a small mountain, offer cultural points of interest such as remains of a medieval castle and surrounding walls. The Vinhais Biological Park also provides panoramic views of the town, adding a cultural dimension to your cycling tour.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1399135195/rebordelo-sanctuary-of-our-lady-of-health-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rebordelo - Sanctuary of Our Lady of Health Circular&lt;/a&gt; (30 km) takes about 1 hour 41 minutes, while the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1401615313/from-valpacos-to-mirandela-circular-along-the-rabacal-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Valpaços to Mirandela — circular along the Rabaçal river&lt;/a&gt; (78.4 km) can take around 3 hours 49 minutes.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the cycling areas around Rebordelo?
A: Information on specific public transport links directly to trailheads in Rebordelo is limited. Given the rural nature of the region, personal vehicles are often the most convenient way to access the starting points of many cycling routes. However, larger towns in the Bragança district may have bus services.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Load for the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Saúde — circular of Rebordelo route?
faqs.answer: This circular racebike route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 42 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 30.0-kilometer (18.6-mile) loop, which includes an elevation gain of about 690 meters (2,264 feet).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces, making it generally easy to ride for road cyclists. It's designed for racebikes, so you can expect smooth roads throughout.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary point of interest is the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Health, which serves as a notable landmark and offers scenic vistas. You'll also enjoy the Surroundings of the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Saúde and various shelters along the road.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route in Rebordelo?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, it's generally recommended to look for parking within the village of Rebordelo, Portugal, as it serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. There are no specific trailhead parking lots mentioned, so local street parking or designated village parking would be the most likely options.

faqs.question: Is the Load for the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Saúde — circular of Rebordelo route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as a 'moderate road ride'. It spans 30.0 kilometers with an elevation gain of around 690 meters, suggesting it requires a good level of fitness. While not extremely challenging, it might be demanding for absolute beginners to road cycling.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the best conditions for road cycling, it's advisable to visit during spring (April-June) or autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather in Portugal is typically milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer or the potentially wetter and colder conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the route in Rebordelo?
faqs.answer: Rebordelo is a small village, and while specific establishments aren't listed, you can typically find local cafes or small restaurants within the village itself. It's a good idea to check locally upon arrival or bring your own snacks and water, especially for longer rides.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on paved surfaces, there are generally no specific restrictions against dogs. However, dogs should always be kept under control, preferably on a leash, and cyclists should be mindful of traffic and other road users. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and pace if they are accompanying you.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee to cycle this route or visit Montesinho Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the 'Load for the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Saúde — circular of Rebordelo' route. Similarly, there are generally no entrance fees for accessing Montesinho Natural Park itself, where the route is located. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, you should pack essentials like a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a phone for navigation or emergencies. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: Are there any challenging sections or notable climbs on the route?
faqs.answer: The route has an elevation gain of approximately 690 meters over 30 kilometers, indicating several climbs. While rated 'moderate,' this elevation gain suggests there will be sustained uphill sections that require a good level of fitness. The climbs offer rewarding scenic views, particularly around the Sanctuary.
